title: malus × atrosanguinea
intro: Malus × atrosanguinea, also known as the Black Blooded Crabapple, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the Rosaceae family. This plant is prized for its deep red or reddish-purple flowers, which are rich in bioactive compounds with potential medicinal properties. The extracts from Malus × atrosanguinea have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as teas and infusions, can be made by steeping its flowers or leaves in hot water, and are said to provide relief from symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory issues.
